questions.question: Can I replant new trees to replace those removed, in lieu of a payment? Am I allowed to plant non-native species as replacements?
questions.answer: You can replant new trees to replace those removed, but the replacement trees must be native to the Northeast United States, excluding invasive species, unless specifically approved as part of a tree replacement plan (§ 281-3). Therefore, planting non-native species as replacements is not generally allowed unless there is specific approval for such a plan.
questions.gap: Consider adding specific guidelines for the monitoring and survival rate of the replanted trees to ensure they thrive, as well as clear replacement ratios to maintain ecological balance.
